Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Continued To Nov-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Gavin for and with Alicia Katz (Petitioner).

The Court noted that Mr. Kelly was not present. Mr. Gavin stated that Mr. Kelly has not filed anything, that Mr. Kelly did not tell him that he was going to be here and that Mr. Kelly indicated that he would not object to the ex parte petition for letters of special administration if he was assured that his Client would get something. The Court noted that they have a 2007 will that appears to revoke everything. Mr. Gavin stated that he thinks the original will is still valid, that the Decedent signed the documents on the same day, that the Petitioner feels it was the Decedent's wish for her and her Sister to split the estate, that the divorce with Alice Katz was final in December of 2006 and the documents were signed in February of 2007. The Court made reference to the 1997 will. Mr. Gavin stated that the Decedent's intent was to leave the estate to Alicia and Shalleen only and that he did not intend to leave anything to any of the other Children. The Court questions what the language of "My Daughter has permission to type the remainder of my will." means in the 2007 document. Ms. Katz stated that it was for her to type that piece of paper, and that it is her typing and her spelling. Mr. Gavin stated that Shalleen fully supports her Sister going forward, that John Derrick has no leads with respect to contacting other Children, that they did additional research to track down school records and that Shalleen signed a waiver of bond. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). The Court will determine which document it will be admitting. Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for October 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Continued To Apr-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Timothy Gavin for and with Alicia M. Katz. Tim Kelly for and with Alice Katz.

Mr. Kelly stated that he was trying to substitute in as of yesterday, that he was requesting a continuance to get up to speed, and that he wasn't sure about whether or not that there would be an objection. The Court stated that some interesting issues have been raised, that the petition seeks to admit the will under the original date, and that the will was republished on two later dates. In addition, the Court stated that a new declaration was submitted, that the filed version of that declaration was not signed, and then inquired about sufficient efforts made to find the disinherited children. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Kelly stated that there are a number of issues, apologized for the inconvenience, and stated that he'd like time to have a clear discussion with his client. Mr. Gavin stated that they believe that the document had no impact, that it doesn't meet the requirements of a codicil, that he needs to submit the codicil to the court/get it on record, and that he did speak with the witnessing attorney. At the request of counsel, the Court took a brief recess on this matter. At the second call of the matter, Mr. Gavin stated that he would like to take a look at the document that the Court has, that there are squatters in the home, and that they may be forced to refile their petition. The Court stated that Mr. Gavin could talk to the clerk's office about the document that he wanted to review. The Court addressed Mr. Gavin stating that the merits weren't before it today, that he could file anything he wants, and that the Court could not give him an advisory opinion. The Court then continued the matter to April 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Continued To Mar-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
